edges.description: Reduced aromatase activity blocks androgen-to-estrogen conversion, lowering estrogen synthesis while leaving androgen precursors relatively excessive.
edges.source: CYP19A1 Loss-of-Function and Aromatase Deficiency

edges.description: Failure of fetal-placental aromatization exposes the pregnant mother to excess androgens, producing antenatal virilization during affected pregnancies.
intermediate_mechanisms: Excess androgens in the fetal-placental unit during pregnancy.
edges.source: Estrogen Deficiency and Androgen Excess

edges.description: Low estrogen weakens reproductive-tissue maturation and negative feedback on the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced estrogen receptor signaling in reproductive tissues and pituitary feedback circuits.
edges.source: Estrogen Deficiency and Androgen Excess
edges.target: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Estrogen deficiency delays epiphyseal closure and impairs bone mineral accrual, causing delayed skeletal maturation and low bone mass.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced estrogen signaling in growth plate and bone remodeling compartments.
edges.source: Estrogen Deficiency and Androgen Excess
edges.target: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

edges.description: Estrogen deficiency can affect glucose-insulin physiology and broader metabolic regulation, although the precise tissue mechanisms remain less resolved than the reproductive and skeletal manifestations.
intermediate_mechanisms: Estrogen-dependent regulation of glucose metabolism and adipose/gonadal signaling.
edges.source: Estrogen Deficiency and Androgen Excess

edges.description: Prenatal androgen excess in 46,XX fetuses produces virilized or ambiguous external genitalia.
intermediate_mechanisms: Fetal-placental aromatase deficiency and high androgen exposure.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Clitoral enlargement is a specific manifestation of 46,XX prenatal and postnatal androgen excess.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Low estrogen feedback contributes to elevated gonadotropins with impaired gonadal function.
intermediate_mechanisms: Loss of estrogen negative feedback on the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Estrogen insufficiency impairs normal pubertal development and secondary sexual-characteristic development.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Estrogen deficiency prevents normal endometrial and pubertal maturation, causing primary amenorrhea in affected 46,XX individuals.
intermediate_mechanisms: Failure of estrogen-dependent uterine and pubertal maturation.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Elevated gonadotropins and ovarian androgens promote ovarian cyst formation in 46,XX aromatase deficiency.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Estrogen deficiency and disrupted ovarian development impair fertility in affected 46,XX individuals.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Estrogen-deficient reproductive and gonadal maturation can impair fertility in affected 46,XY individuals.
edges.source: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

edges.description: Lack of estrogen-mediated epiphyseal maturation delays bone age.
edges.source: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

edges.description: Impaired estrogen-dependent epiphyseal maturation delays epiphyseal ossification.
edges.source: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

edges.description: Delayed epiphyseal closure permits persistent linear growth into adulthood, particularly in 46,XY affected individuals.
intermediate_mechanisms: Persistent growth plate activity from lack of estrogen-mediated epiphyseal closure.
edges.source: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

edges.description: Impaired estrogen-dependent bone remodeling and peak bone mass accrual cause osteopenia or osteoporosis.
edges.source: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

edges.description: Reduced estrogen signaling can impair bone mass accrual before reaching the osteoporosis threshold.
edges.source: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

edges.description: Estrogen-deficiency effects on glucose metabolism can manifest as reduced insulin sensitivity.
edges.source: Metabolic Effects of Estrogen Deficiency
Estrogen Replacement Therapy
Estrogen Replacement Therapy
edges.target: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

edges.description: Very low or undetectable estradiol reports the aromatase enzyme block.
edges.source: CYP19A1 Loss-of-Function and Aromatase Deficiency

edges.description: Low serum estradiol directly reports the estrogen-deficient endocrine state.
edges.source: Estrogen Deficiency and Androgen Excess

edges.description: Normal-to-elevated circulating testosterone reports androgen excess from failed aromatization.
edges.source: Estrogen Deficiency and Androgen Excess
CYP19A1

nodes.description: Virilized or atypical external genitalia in 46,XX individuals caused by prenatal androgen excess when placental/fetal aromatization is deficient.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency: A case series of 46, XX Chinese children and a systematic review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The main characteristics were maternal antenatal virilization (21/29), ambiguous genitalia (mainly Prader IV or III, 19/23), delayed bone age (16/17), low bone mass (5/8), markedly elevated FSH levels and ovarian cysts (13/30).

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency due to novel CYP19A1 mutation: a rare cause of maternal and fetal virilization.
evidence.snippet: Aromatase deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive condition due to a mutation in the CYP19A1 encoding aromatase enzyme.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency in an Ontario Old Order Mennonite family.
evidence.snippet: Our proband is an Old Order Mennonite female born with ambiguous genitalia who was identified to carry novel homozygous variant in the CYP19A1 gene c.1304G>A (p. Arg435His).

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in CYP19A1 reduce or abolish aromatase activity. Aromatase normally catalyzes androgen-to-estrogen conversion in the placenta, gonads, adipose tissue, and other estrogen-producing tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency caused by mutation of CYP19A1 gene: A case report.
evidence.snippet: Aromatase deficiency (AD) is a rare autosomal recessive genetic disease caused by loss-of-function mutations in aromatase gene (CYP19A1), leading to congenital estrogen deficiency syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of a novel CYP19A1 (aromatase) R192H mutation causing virilization of a 46,XX newborn, undervirilization of the 46,XY brother, but no virilization of the mother during pregnancies.
evidence.snippet: P450 aromatase (CYP19A1) is essential for the biosynthesis of estrogens from androgen precursors. Mutations in the coding region of CYP19A1 lead to autosomal recessive aromatase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: [Aromatase deficiency].
evidence.snippet: Aromatase, a key enzyme in estrogen synthesis, is tissue-specifically regulated in various tissues and plays an important role through endocrine and intracrine estrogen production in various physiological functions.

nodes.description: Enlarged clitoris due to androgen excess in 46,XX individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency: an unusual cause for primary amenorrhea with virilization.
evidence.snippet: She lacked feminine secondary sexual characters, had eunuchoid body habitus and prominent clitoromegaly.

nodes.description: Poor or delayed development of secondary sexual characteristics due to estrogen deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency and estrogen resistance: from molecular genetics to clinic.
evidence.snippet: At puberty, affected girls have hypergonadotropic hypogonadism, fail to develop secondary sexual characteristics, and exhibit progressive virilization.

nodes.description: Delayed bone age and persistent open epiphyses due to insufficient estrogen-mediated skeletal maturation.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency: A case series of 46, XX Chinese children and a systematic review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The main characteristics were maternal antenatal virilization (21/29), ambiguous genitalia (mainly Prader IV or III, 19/23), delayed bone age (16/17), low bone mass (5/8), markedly elevated FSH levels and ovarian cysts (13/30).

nodes.description: Epiphyseal ossification is delayed when estrogen-dependent skeletal maturation and epiphyseal closure are impaired.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency
evidence.snippet: HP:0002663 | Delayed epiphyseal ossification | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Loss of aromatase causes systemic estrogen deficiency and relative androgen excess. This disrupts genital differentiation, puberty, gonadotropin feedback, skeletal maturation, bone mass accrual, and metabolic regulation.

evidence.reference_title: Human models of aromatase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Human congenital estrogen deficiency, due to an inactivating mutation of the aromatase gene, leads to the lack of the estrogen synthesis, with gonadotropins and circulating testosterone ranging from normal to elevated.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic and clinical spectrum of aromatase deficiency in infancy, childhood and adolescence.
evidence.snippet: After birth, the dual effect of aromatase deficiency, excessive androgens, and insufficient estrogens is responsible for a variable clinical picture.

nodes.description: Estradiol replacement restores estrogen exposure, supports skeletal maturation and epiphyseal closure, and is the central disease-directed therapy once aromatase deficiency is diagnosed.

evidence.reference_title: Human models of aromatase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The estradiol replacement treatment leads to a complete epiphyseal closure and to the skeletal maturation.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency in men: a clinical perspective.
evidence.snippet: Transdermal estradiol treatment at a daily dose of about 25 microg might be adequate for lifelong replacement therapy.

nodes.description: Infertility in affected 46,XX individuals reflects estrogen-deficient reproductive development and ovarian dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency
evidence.snippet: HP:0008222 | Female infertility | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Insufficient estrogen signaling disrupts pubertal maturation and removes normal gonadotropin feedback, while androgen excess drives virilization in 46,XX individuals. Elevated FSH and ovarian cysts are frequent diagnostic clues in 46,XX aromatase deficiency.
nodes.id: Gonadotropin Feedback and Reproductive Development Disruption

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency and estrogen resistance: from molecular genetics to clinic.
evidence.snippet: It gives rise to ambiguous genitalia in 46,XX individuals. At puberty, affected girls have hypergonadotropic hypogonadism, fail to develop secondary sexual characteristics, and exhibit progressive virilization.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency: A case series of 46, XX Chinese children and a systematic review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The main characteristics were maternal antenatal virilization (21/29), ambiguous genitalia (mainly Prader IV or III, 19/23), delayed bone age (16/17), low bone mass (5/8), markedly elevated FSH levels and ovarian cysts (13/30).

nodes.description: Elevated gonadotropin levels with inadequate gonadal function due to loss of estrogen feedback and impaired reproductive maturation.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency and estrogen resistance: from molecular genetics to clinic.
evidence.snippet: At puberty, affected girls have hypergonadotropic hypogonadism, fail to develop secondary sexual characteristics, and exhibit progressive virilization.

nodes.description: Estrogen deficiency delays epiphyseal closure and skeletal maturation, extends linear growth, and impairs attainment of peak bone mass, producing tall stature, unfused epiphyses, delayed bone age, osteopenia, and osteoporosis.
nodes.id: Impaired Skeletal Maturation and Bone Remodeling

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase activity and bone loss.
evidence.snippet: Several lines of clinical and experimental evidence now clearly indicate that aromatase activity and estrogen production are necessary for longitudinal bone growth, attainment of peak bone mass, the pubertal growth spurt, epiphyseal closure, and normal bone remodeling in young individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Human models of aromatase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The clinical phenotype in the male affected subjects comprises tall stature, persistent linear growth and delayed bone age, osteopenia/osteoporosis, eunuchoid body proportion, different degrees of glucose-insulin and of fertility impairment.

nodes.description: Reduced insulin sensitivity or glucose-insulin impairment associated with congenital estrogen deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic and clinical spectrum of aromatase deficiency in infancy, childhood and adolescence.
evidence.snippet: Insulin sensitivity may be abnormal in both men and women.

nodes.description: Infertility in affected 46,XY individuals is part of the reproductive phenotype of congenital estrogen deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency
evidence.snippet: HP:0003251 | Male infertility | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Virilization of the pregnant mother can occur when the fetal-placental unit cannot aromatize androgen precursors to estrogens, leading to excess androgen exposure during affected pregnancies.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency: A case series of 46, XX Chinese children and a systematic review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The main characteristics were maternal antenatal virilization (21/29), ambiguous genitalia (mainly Prader IV or III, 19/23), delayed bone age (16/17), low bone mass (5/8), markedly elevated FSH levels and ovarian cysts (13/30).

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency due to novel CYP19A1 mutation: a rare cause of maternal and fetal virilization.
evidence.snippet: A 10-day-old newborn presented with atypical genitalia and a history of maternal virilization during pregnancy.

nodes.description: Aromatase-deficient individuals can show abnormalities of glucose-insulin physiology. The clinical literature supports metabolic involvement, but the precise intermediates between congenital estrogen deficiency and insulin resistance remain incompletely defined.

evidence.reference_title: Human models of aromatase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: These phenotypes suggest the physiological role of estrogens on the skeleton, on pituitary function, on the reproductive system, on glucose metabolism, being the precise mechanism on each of these functions not yet known in detail.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic and clinical spectrum of aromatase deficiency in infancy, childhood and adolescence.
evidence.snippet: Insulin sensitivity may be abnormal in both men and women.

nodes.description: Reduced bone mineral density may present as osteopenia in aromatase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency
evidence.snippet: HP:0000938 | Osteopenia | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Reduced bone mineral density and increased fracture risk resulting from inadequate estrogen-mediated bone remodeling and peak bone mass accrual.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency: A case series of 46, XX Chinese children and a systematic review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The main characteristics were maternal antenatal virilization (21/29), ambiguous genitalia (mainly Prader IV or III, 19/23), delayed bone age (16/17), low bone mass (5/8), markedly elevated FSH levels and ovarian cysts (13/30).

evidence.reference_title: Human models of aromatase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The clinical phenotype in the male affected subjects comprises tall stature, persistent linear growth and delayed bone age, osteopenia/osteoporosis, eunuchoid body proportion, different degrees of glucose-insulin and of fertility impairment.

nodes.description: Multiple ovarian cysts associated with elevated gonadotropins and ovarian androgen excess in 46,XX aromatase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency: A case series of 46, XX Chinese children and a systematic review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: The main characteristics were maternal antenatal virilization (21/29), ambiguous genitalia (mainly Prader IV or III, 19/23), delayed bone age (16/17), low bone mass (5/8), markedly elevated FSH levels and ovarian cysts (13/30).

nodes.description: Absence of menarche due to estrogen deficiency preventing normal pubertal and endometrial development.

evidence.reference_title: Human models of aromatase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: At puberty they show primary amenorrhea, no breast development, worsening of the virilization and the absence of growth spurt.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency caused by mutation of CYP19A1 gene: A case report.
evidence.snippet: His estradiol was below the measurable line, the follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) increased, bone age delayed, epiphysis unfused, and the bone mass reduced.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency in men: a clinical perspective.
evidence.snippet: Diagnosis of the condition is supported by the presence of unfused epiphyses and undetectable serum estradiol levels; the condition can be further substantiated by genetic sequencing of CYP19A1.

readouts.interpretation: Very low or undetectable estradiol reports the aromatase enzyme block.
Low serum estradiol directly reports the estrogen-deficient endocrine state.

evidence.reference_title: Human models of aromatase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Human congenital estrogen deficiency, due to an inactivating mutation of the aromatase gene, leads to the lack of the estrogen synthesis, with gonadotropins and circulating testosterone ranging from normal to elevated.

readouts.interpretation: Normal-to-elevated circulating testosterone reports androgen excess from failed aromatization.

nodes.description: Continued linear growth beyond the usual age of epiphyseal closure, especially in 46,XY affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Aromatase deficiency and estrogen resistance: from molecular genetics to clinic.
evidence.snippet: These men are extremely tall and have eunuchoid proportions with continued linear growth into adulthood, lack of epiphyseal closure, and osteoporosis due to estrogen deficiency.
